proposalSummary.overview: Elitery proposes to deliver a fully managed, 24/7 Security Operations Center (SOC) service for PT Sentosa Abadi Finansial. The solution combines a cloud-native SIEM, continuous monitoring of cloud and on-premise systems, and structured incident response operating entirely from Indonesian data centers. The service will transition through structured phases including assessment and onboarding, managed by a dedicated certified security team.

completenessChecklist.note: Section 1 outlines Elitery's proposal to operate a 24/7 Managed Security Operations Center for PT Sentosa Abadi Finansial, highlighting risk reduction, regulatory compliance, and local data residency.
Section 3 details the scope of work, including 24/7 security monitoring, SIEM deployment and management, incident response, reporting, threat intelligence, data residency, and compliance alignment.
Section 4 outlines a four-phase delivery methodology (Assess, Onboard, Operate, Improve) and describes the shift-based analyst structure.
Section 5 outlines high-level transition phases but lacks specific dates, durations, or milestones, deferring the detailed schedule to contract finalization.
There is no pricing or cost breakdown section or mention of commercial terms in the proposal.
The proposal does not contain a dedicated section or substantive details regarding assumptions or exclusions.

Section 6 lists the proposed roles for the dedicated delivery team (Service Delivery Manager, SOC Lead, Incident Response Lead, Security Analysts, Detection Engineer) and mentions their key certifications (CISSP, OSCP, GCIH).

Section 2 provides an overview of Elitery, established in 2011, specializing in managed cloud, disaster recovery, and managed security services, operating from Tier III data centers in Jakarta and Surabaya.

Section 7 highlights Elitery's experience since 2018 and lists representative engagements with a national multi-finance company and a regional commercial bank, noting references are available upon request.

Section 8 commits to the incident-response service levels described in section 3.3 (15-minute acknowledgment and 30-minute containment for critical incidents) but notes detailed SLAs will be documented in the final agreement.
The proposal does not contain a terms and conditions section.
The proposal does not contain a risk management section.
Section 9 summarizes Elitery's differentiators, including Indonesian data-center sovereignty, ISO 27001-certified management system, and an experienced security team.

items.requirement: The initial contract term shall be twelve (12) months, commencing on the agreed service-commencement date.
items.note: The proposal does not mention the initial contract term of twelve (12) months.

items.requirement: The contract shall include an option to extend for two further periods of twelve (12) months each, subject to satisfactory performance.
items.note: The proposal does not mention any option to extend the contract.

items.requirement: The provider must deliver 24x7x365 security monitoring of Sentosa's cloud and on-premise production environment from a staffed Security Operations Center.
items.note: Elitery proposes 24/7 security monitoring of Sentosa's cloud and on-premise production systems from its staffed Security Operations Center.

items.requirement: The provider must perform deployment, tuning, and ongoing management of a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platform.
items.note: Elitery will deploy and operate a cloud-native SIEM platform, including tuning and ongoing management.

items.requirement: The provider must perform log onboarding for all in-scope systems into the SIEM platform.
items.note: Elitery will onboard log sources across Sentosa's in-scope systems into the SIEM platform.

items.requirement: The provider must deliver incident response with documented service levels.
items.note: Elitery operates a documented incident-response process with committed service levels.

items.requirement: The provider must acknowledge CRITICAL incidents within 15 minutes, 24 hours a day.
items.note: Elitery commits to acknowledge CRITICAL incidents within 15 minutes, 24 hours a day.

items.requirement: The provider must commence containment of CRITICAL incidents within 30 minutes, 24 hours a day.
items.note: Elitery commits to begin containment of CRITICAL incidents within 30 minutes, 24 hours a day.

items.requirement: The provider must perform quarterly vulnerability assessment of Sentosa's public-facing applications.
items.note: The proposal does not mention quarterly vulnerability assessments.

items.requirement: The provider must perform quarterly penetration testing of Sentosa's public-facing applications.
items.note: The proposal does not mention quarterly penetration testing.

items.requirement: The provider must perform remediation tracking for vulnerability assessments and penetration testing.
items.note: The proposal does not mention remediation tracking for vulnerability assessments or penetration testing.

items.requirement: The provider should deliver a monthly executive security report.
items.note: Elitery will provide a monthly executive security report.

items.requirement: The provider should conduct a quarterly service-review meeting with Sentosa management.
items.note: Elitery will hold a quarterly service-review meeting with Sentosa management.

items.requirement: The provider must demonstrate support for Sentosa's regulatory compliance, specifically OJK security requirements for financial technology operators.
items.note: Elitery's proposed SOC service and reporting are designed to support Sentosa's regulatory compliance as an OJK-supervised financial technology operator.

items.requirement: The provider must demonstrate support for the ISO/IEC 27001 control framework.
items.note: Elitery holds ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification and its SOC operates under an ISO/IEC 27001-certified management system.

items.requirement: All customer and security log data must be stored within data-center facilities located in Indonesia.
items.note: All Sentosa log and security data will be stored exclusively within Elitery's Indonesian data-center facilities.

items.requirement: All customer and security log data must be processed within data-center facilities located in Indonesia.
items.note: All Sentosa log and security data will be processed exclusively within Elitery's Indonesian data-center facilities.

items.requirement: The provider should integrate at least one commercial or government threat-intelligence feed into the monitoring platform.
items.note: Elitery integrates commercial and community threat-intelligence feeds into its monitoring platform.

items.requirement: The provider should deliver security-awareness training for Sentosa staff, at least twice per contract year.
items.note: The proposal does not mention security-awareness training for Sentosa staff.

items.requirement: Service transition and onboarding should be completed within eight (8) weeks of contract signature.
items.note: The proposal does not specify that service transition and onboarding will be completed within eight (8) weeks.

items.requirement: The provider should deliver a documented transition plan for service transition and onboarding.
items.note: The proposal does not mention delivering a documented transition plan.

items.requirement: The provider should provide a named, dedicated account manager.
items.note: The proposal does not provide a named, dedicated account manager.

items.requirement: The provider should provide a documented escalation matrix.
items.note: The proposal does not mention a documented escalation matrix.

items.requirement: The provider must demonstrate at least three (3) years of experience delivering comparable managed SOC services.
items.note: Elitery has delivered managed security services since 2018, demonstrating over seven years of experience.

items.requirement: The provider's experience must be supported by at least two client references in the financial sector.
items.note: The proposal mentions representative engagements with a national multi-finance company and a regional commercial bank, but states that client references will be provided during the evaluation process rather than including them in the proposal.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain an executive summary.
items.note: The proposal contains an Executive Summary in Section 1.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain a description of the provider's company, including relevant certifications.
items.note: The proposal contains a Company Profile in Section 2, including its ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain a scope of work that addresses every requirement R1-R12.
items.note: The proposal contains a Scope of Work in Section 3, but it does not address all requirements R1-R12 (R1, R2, R9, R10, and R11 are missing).

items.requirement: The proposal must contain the proposed methodology and service-delivery approach.
items.note: The proposal contains the proposed methodology and service-delivery approach in Section 4.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain a project timeline covering transition and steady-state operations, with dated milestones.
items.note: The proposal contains a project timeline in Section 5, but it does not include dated milestones.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain the proposed team, including roles, named key personnel, and certifications.
items.note: The proposal contains the proposed team and roles in Section 6, but does not name the key personnel.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain a complete and itemised price schedule.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a price schedule.

items.requirement: The price schedule must state one-time transition fees.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a price schedule, so one-time transition fees are not stated.

items.requirement: The price schedule must state recurring monthly fees.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a price schedule, so recurring monthly fees are not stated.

items.requirement: The price schedule must state applicable taxes.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a price schedule, so applicable taxes are not stated.

items.requirement: The price schedule must state the total 12-month contract value in Indonesian Rupiah.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a price schedule, so the total 12-month contract value in Indonesian Rupiah is not stated.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain a clear statement of all assumptions, dependencies, and exclusions on which the proposal and price are based.
items.note: The proposal does not contain a statement of assumptions, dependencies, or exclusions.

items.requirement: The proposal must contain relevant client references and case studies.
items.note: The proposal mentions representative engagements but does not contain detailed case studies or actual client references.

items.requirement: Proposals must be submitted in PDF format.
items.note: The proposal does not mention the submission format.

items.requirement: Proposals must be submitted by 30 May 2026.
items.note: The proposal is dated 22 May 2026, which is before the submission deadline of 30 May 2026.

keyGaps.description: Mandatory section "Assumptions & Exclusions" is missing — the proposal cannot be submitted without it.
Mandatory section "Pricing / Cost Breakdown" is missing — the proposal cannot be submitted without it.
The scope of SIEM deployment and log onboarding is undefined. There are no limits specified for the number of in-scope systems, endpoints, or log sources to be onboarded.
Detailed service-level definitions for monitoring availability and reporting are not provided and are deferred to the final service agreement, creating ambiguity on operational commitments.

commercialRisks.description: The Pricing / Cost Breakdown section is completely absent from the proposal. A bid with no price is non-responsive and cannot be commercially evaluated.

The Assumptions & Exclusions section is completely absent. This leaves the boundaries of Elitery's responsibilities completely undefined, creating a massive risk of misunderstandings regarding client prerequisites and out-of-scope tasks.

Cloud-native SIEM licensing and ingestion costs are not addressed. Because log volume limits (e.g., GB/day or Events Per Second) and licensing ownership are not defined, there is a high risk of massive cost overruns if log ingestion exceeds unstated expectations.

The transition timeline is completely uncommitted, stating only that Elitery will 'agree the detailed schedule during contract finalization.' This introduces significant schedule risk and potential project delays.

The proposal commits to a 30-minute containment SLA for critical incidents without defining the legal and operational boundaries of 'containment' (e.g., whether Elitery has active authorization to isolate client infrastructure/endpoints, or if they only provide advisory containment steps).

valueProposition.note: Elitery presents a solid technical value proposition tailored to Indonesian financial regulations (OJK), highlighting local data residency (Jakarta/Surabaya Tier III DCs), ISO 27001 certification, and aggressive incident response SLAs (15-min ack / 30-min containment). However, the complete lack of commercial details, pricing, and scope boundaries severely undermines the overall strength and viability of the proposal.

recommendations.action: Create and insert a comprehensive "Pricing / Cost Breakdown" section. This section must be itemized and include: one-time transition fees, recurring monthly fees, applicable taxes, and the total 12-month contract value stated in Indonesian Rupiah (IDR).
recommendations.rationale: The proposal currently lacks any pricing information, which makes it non-responsive and impossible to evaluate commercially.

recommendations.action: Create and insert a dedicated "Assumptions & Exclusions" section. This section must clearly outline all assumptions, dependencies, client prerequisites, and out-of-scope tasks to establish clear boundaries for Elitery's responsibilities.
recommendations.rationale: Without this section, the boundaries of Elitery's responsibilities are completely undefined, creating a massive risk of scope creep and misunderstandings.

recommendations.action: Update the Scope of Work to explicitly include and describe the delivery of quarterly vulnerability assessments, quarterly penetration testing of public-facing applications, remediation tracking for both assessments, and security-awareness training for Sentosa staff at least twice per contract year.
recommendations.rationale: These are mandatory technical and training requirements from the RFP that are currently completely missing from the proposal's scope.

recommendations.action: Add a documented transition plan and a detailed project timeline with dated milestones covering transition and steady-state operations. Explicitly commit to completing the service transition and onboarding within eight (8) weeks of contract signature, replacing the vague statement about agreeing on a schedule during contract finalization.
recommendations.rationale: The RFP requires a documented transition plan and a committed 8-week timeline. Leaving this uncommitted introduces significant schedule risk.

recommendations.action: Add a section defining the contract term as an initial twelve (12) months commencing on the service-commencement date, with an option to extend for two further periods of twelve (12) months each, subject to satisfactory performance.
recommendations.rationale: These contract duration and extension options are mandatory RFP requirements that must be explicitly agreed to in the proposal.

recommendations.action: Add details of the proposed team, including specific roles, named key personnel, their professional certifications, a named dedicated account manager, and a documented escalation matrix.
recommendations.rationale: The RFP requires named key personnel, certifications, a dedicated account manager, and an escalation matrix, which are currently missing or only partially addressed.

recommendations.action: Ensure the final proposal document is exported and submitted in PDF format.
recommendations.rationale: The RFP explicitly mandates that proposals must be submitted in PDF format.

recommendations.action: Define the exact scope of the SIEM deployment and log onboarding, including specific limits on the number of in-scope systems, endpoints, or log sources. Explicitly address cloud-native SIEM licensing and ingestion costs, specifying log volume limits (e.g., GB/day or EPS) and licensing ownership.
recommendations.rationale: Leaving SIEM ingestion and licensing undefined creates a high risk of massive cost overruns and scope creep.

recommendations.action: Provide detailed service-level definitions for monitoring availability and reporting. Clearly define the legal and operational boundaries of the "30-minute containment SLA" for critical incidents, specifying whether Elitery has active authorization to isolate client infrastructure/endpoints or if they only provide advisory containment steps.
recommendations.rationale: Deferring SLA definitions to the final agreement creates operational ambiguity, and an undefined containment SLA poses significant legal and operational risks.

recommendations.action: Update the client references and case studies section to include at least two relevant client references specifically from the financial sector.
recommendations.rationale: The RFP requires at least two client references in the financial sector to support Elitery's experience, which is currently only partially met.

readinessScore.rationale: Verdict hard-capped at NOT_READY — mandatory section(s) missing: Pricing / Cost Breakdown, Assumptions & Exclusions; 2 other critical issue(s). A score of 49/100 cannot raise the verdict while blocking issues remain.
